# Feature: Collection Operations Integrity ‚Äî Rename/Delete/Move Persistence, ID Retention, Validations

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-003
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

Make collection CRUD trustworthy. Today: renaming a collection in the VS Code extension silently doesn't persist (the `saveCollection` message is dropped ‚Äî no handler case exists); moving or duplicating a request regenerates its `id` and drops item-level fields; and the Zustand store keys update/remove operations on `info.name` ‚Äî the very field that changes during rename. After this feature: every mutation reports real success/failure, item and request IDs are stable across rename/move/duplicate, the store and services are keyed by `filename`/`waveId`/`item.id` (never display name), and all save/import paths enforce name validations with descriptive errors.

**Verified root causes** (all confirmed in current code):
1. [vsCodeAdapter.ts:374-380](../../packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts) ‚Äî `saveCollection` fires a postMessage and optimistically returns `ok(collection)`; `MessageHandler.handleMessage` has no `saveCollection` case, so the message is silently dropped.
2. [CollectionService.ts:330-342](../../packages/shared/src/services/CollectionService.ts) ‚Äî `saveRequest` matches by **name** and pushes new items with fresh `generateUniqueId()`; both adapters ([vsCodeAdapter.ts:392-400](../../packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts), [webAdapter.ts:466-474](../../packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts)) serialize only `item.request` + `item.name`, dropping `item.id`/`description`/`response`.
3. [createCollectionsSlice.tsx:33-38](../../packages/core/src/hooks/store/createCollectionsSlice.tsx) ‚Äî `updateCollection`/`removeCollection` match on `c.info.name`.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No persisted-shape changes (no schema version bump). This feature enforces **invariants** over the existing shapes:

### Entities & Invariants

- **Collection**: `filename` (string) ‚Äî the persistence key, always present after load; `info.waveId` (string) ‚Äî logical identity; `info.name` ‚Äî display only, **never a lookup key** after this feature.
- **CollectionItem**: `id` (string) ‚Äî stable across rename, move, duplicate-source; never regenerated when one already exists. When `item.request` is present: `item.name === item.request.name` (atomic rename invariant) and `item.request.id` is likewise stable.
- **Validation rules** (every save/import path):
  - item/collection name: non-empty after trim
  - sibling-level uniqueness (case-insensitive) among folders and requests at the same tree level
  - collection-level: `info.name` unique (case-insensitive) across all loaded collections
  - every item in the tree has an `id`

### Relationships

- Wire contract change (not persisted shape): `saveRequestToCollection` carries the **whole `CollectionItem`** (`id`, `name`, `description?`, `request`, `response?`) instead of `(requestContent, requestName)` ‚Äî through the vscode message, the server route body, and `CollectionService.saveRequest`.

### Indexes & Constraints

- Store slice operations keyed by `filename`; per memory ("backwards compatibility is not required"), signatures change cleanly ‚Äî no dual-key support.

## 3. Files

- `packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts` ‚Äî new `saveCollection` case + handler
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts` ‚Äî `saveCollection` ‚Üí `sendAndWait`; `saveRequestToCollection` sends full item
- `packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts` ‚Äî `saveRequestToCollection` sends full item
- `packages/server/src/routes/collections.ts` ‚Äî request-save route accepts full item payload
- `packages/shared/src/services/CollectionService.ts` ‚Äî `saveRequest` signature/logic (id-first matching, id retention, validations)
- `packages/core/src/hooks/store/createCollectionsSlice.tsx` ‚Äî re-key `updateCollection`/`removeCollection` by `filename`
- `packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionsPane.tsx` ‚Äî slice-caller updates; rename/duplicate name-integrity
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/App.tsx`, `packages/web/src/App.tsx` ‚Äî slice-caller updates
- `packages/core/src/utils/collectionParser.ts` ‚Äî `renameItemInTree` hardened (request.name sync); shared validation util (e.g., `validateCollectionTree` / `validateItemName`)
- `packages/vscode/src/test/handlers/MessageHandler.test.ts` ‚Äî regression test for `saveCollection`
- `packages/core/src/test/hooks/store/createCollectionsSlice.test.tsx` (or existing slice test location) ‚Äî re-key tests
- `packages/shared/src/test/services/CollectionService.test.ts` ‚Äî id-retention + validation tests
- `packages/core/src/test/utils/collectionParser.test.ts` ‚Äî rename/validation helper tests
- `docs/features/collections.md` ‚Äî note rename/move semantics and validation rules

## 4. Dependencies

- None hard. FEAT-001's logging/validation work touches the same `CollectionService` ‚Äî coordinate merge order but no functional dependency. FEAT-004 (move dialog) builds **on top of** this feature's id-retention.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Fix vscode collection persistence ‚Äî `saveCollection` handler + `sendAndWait`

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add a `saveCollection` case to `MessageHandler.handleMessage` ‚Üí new `handleSaveCollection(message)`: parse `message.data.collection`, require `collection.filename` (error response if absent), call `collectionService.save(collection, collection.filename)`, reply with the standard `{ requestId, collection }` success / `{ requestId, error }` failure shape. Convert `vsCodeAdapter.saveCollection` from fire-and-forget to `sendAndWait<Collection>('saveCollection', ‚Ä¶)` (add `'saveCollection': 'collection'` to the `responseDataMap`), so the UI receives real success/failure instead of the optimistic `ok`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: **Regression-first** (per plan testing strategy): `MessageHandler.test.ts` ‚Äî `saveCollection` message persists via `collectionService.save` and replies with the saved collection; missing `filename` replies with a descriptive error; service throw replies with error. Adapter-level: response with `error` resolves to `err`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `handleSaveCollection`; note in `docs/platforms/vscode.md` is not needed (internal fix).
- **Verify**: In the VS Code extension, rename a collection, reload the window ‚Äî the new name persists; a forced service failure surfaces an error notification instead of silent success.

### TASK-002: Re-key the collections slice by `filename`

- [ ] **Implementation**: Change `createCollectionsSlice` signatures to `removeCollection(filename: string)` and `updateCollection(filename: string, updates: Partial<Collection>)`, matching on `c.filename`. Update every caller: `CollectionsPane.tsx` (rename, delete, item-mutation flows) and both `App.tsx` files (search for `updateCollection(`/`removeCollection(` call sites). Where a caller currently passes `info.name`, it has `collection` in scope ‚Äî pass `collection.filename!` (collections in the store always come from `loadAll`, which sets `filename`).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Slice tests ‚Äî `updateCollection` by filename updates the right collection even when `info.name` changed in the same update (the rename case that broke before); `removeCollection` removes by filename; collections with identical display names are tracked independently.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the slice interface documenting "filename is the store key; never key on display name" (plan architecture rule).
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test` green; rename a collection in either app ‚Äî the tree updates the correct entry (no duplicate/ghost entry).
- **Depends on**: TASK-001 (rename persistence must work to verify end-to-end)

### TASK-003: ID retention ‚Äî `saveRequestToCollection` carries the whole `CollectionItem`

- [ ] **Implementation**: End-to-end contract change:
  - **Adapters** (`vsCodeAdapter`, `webAdapter`): send `item: JSON.stringify(item)` (full `CollectionItem`: id, name, description, request, response) instead of `requestContent` + `requestName`.
  - **MessageHandler** + **server route** (`POST /api/collections/:filename/requests`): accept the `item` payload and pass through.
  - **`CollectionService.saveRequest`**: new signature `saveRequest(itemContent: string, collectionFileName: string, folderPath: string[], newCollectionName?: string)` ‚Äî parse the `CollectionItem`, sanitize via existing `sanitizeRequestForSave(item.request)`, then match the target slot by **`id` first** (update in place, preserving the incoming item's fields), falling back to name match only if no id matches; **never** regenerate an id that already exists ‚Äî `generateUniqueId()` only when the incoming item lacks one. Per memory, remove the old signature cleanly (no legacy params).
  - Set `item.request.name = item.name` defensively before persisting (invariant).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `CollectionService.test.ts` ‚Äî saving an item with existing `id` into a new folder keeps `item.id` and `request.id`; re-saving same id updates in place (no duplicate); item with same name but different id at target does **not** get overwritten by name-fallback when ids are present; `description`/`response` fields survive the round-trip; new item without id gets one. Cross-platform: MessageHandler routing test + webAdapter test for the new payload shape (plan testing strategy).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `saveRequest` documenting id-first matching; update `IStorageAdapter.saveRequestToCollection` JSDoc in `adapters.ts`.
- **Verify**: Move a request between folders (UI) and inspect the collection JSON on disk ‚Äî the item keeps its original `id`; duplicate-then-rename flows don't collide.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002

### TASK-004: Atomic name integrity in rename/duplicate helpers

- [ ] **Implementation**: Harden `renameItemInTree` ([collectionParser.ts:302](../../packages/core/src/utils/collectionParser.ts)) to set both `item.name` **and**, when `item.request` exists, `item.request.name` to the new name in the same immutable update. Audit `duplicateRequestItem` and any other tree-mutation helpers in `collectionParser.ts` for the same invariant (duplicated items must get fresh `id`s for item *and* request, with `name === request.name`).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `collectionParser` tests ‚Äî rename of a request item updates both names; rename of a folder leaves child request names untouched; duplicate produces fresh ids but synced names; rename of nested item works at depth.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc invariant note on the helpers (`CollectionItem.name === request.name` when request present).
- **Verify**: After renaming a request in the tree, open it in a tab ‚Äî the tab/request name matches; collection JSON on disk shows both names updated.

### TASK-005: Shared save/import validations with descriptive `Result` errors

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add a shared validation util in `packages/core/src/utils/collectionParser.ts` (e.g., `validateItemName(name, siblings, excludeId): Result<string, string>` returning the trimmed name, and `validateCollectionTree(collection): Result<void, string>` checking: non-empty trimmed names everywhere; case-insensitive sibling-level uniqueness for folders and requests; all items have ids). `CollectionsPane.tsx` already does ad-hoc sibling checks ([CollectionsPane.tsx:367-375](../../packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionsPane.tsx)) ‚Äî replace with the shared util. Enforce collection-level `info.name` uniqueness (case-insensitive across loaded collections) at the create/rename entry points in `CollectionsPane.tsx`/wizards. Server-side: `CollectionService.saveRequest`/`save` run `validateCollectionTree` before writing and reject with descriptive errors (surfaced via the existing error‚Üínotification path).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: util tests ‚Äî empty/whitespace name rejected; case-insensitive duplicate sibling rejected (excluding self by id); valid tree passes; tree with missing id fails naming the path. Service tests ‚Äî save rejected on duplicate sibling names; error message names the offending item.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: `docs/features/collections.md` ‚Äî document the naming rules (non-empty, unique among siblings, unique collection names).
- **Verify**: Attempt to rename a request to a sibling's name (different case) ‚Äî error notification, no write; import is unaffected for valid files.
- **Depends on**: TASK-003, TASK-004

### TASK-006: Folder vs. request operation parity audit

- [ ] **Implementation**: Verify (and fix where needed) that rename/delete behave identically for folders and requests: folder delete removes the subtree (existing `deleteItem` ‚Äî confirm); folder **rename** updates dependent path consumers ‚Äî expanded-state keys (`savedExpandedFolders` in the slice / `CollectionsPane` expansion keys) and open tabs whose `sourceRef.itemPath` includes the renamed folder (update the path segment so save-back targets the right folder). Trace `sourceRef.itemPath` consumers from `CollectionReference` usages.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: rename folder ‚Üí expanded-state key for that folder follows the new name (or is harmlessly reset); a tab whose `sourceRef.itemPath` contains the old folder name gets the updated path; folder delete removes all descendants from the store.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Inline comments at the path-update sites explaining why folder rename must touch `sourceRef`.
- **Verify**: Rename a folder containing an open tab's request, then Ctrl+save the tab ‚Äî it saves into the renamed folder, not a recreated old-name folder.
- **Depends on**: TASK-004

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: All new failure paths return descriptive errors through the existing channels: services throw descriptive `Error`s ‚Üí MessageHandler/route catch ‚Üí error response ‚Üí adapter `Result.err` ‚Üí `notification.showNotification('error', ‚Ä¶)`. The `vsCodeAdapter.saveCollection` conversion removes the last optimistic-`ok` mutation (plan architecture rule: request/response over fire-and-forget for mutations).
- **Logging**: Per the plan's logging strategy, log every collection mutation failure in `CollectionService`/`MessageHandler` with context (filename, waveId/item id, operation) ‚Äî these were silent before. Never log request bodies/headers.
